The Brassica genus, commonly referred to as the Crucifera family, is a group of flowering plants known for their cruciform (cross-shaped) flowers and pungent aromas. The health benefits of plants in this genus include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Brassica species include glucosinolates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-cancer, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects.
